activities_projects.title: The Entitlement Approach: Sen’s Famine Theory
activities_projects.objective: Analyze famines as a failure of 'Exchange Entitlements' rather than just food availability
steps: Investigate Amartya Sen’s theory that the Bengal Famine was caused by a 'Failure of Entitlements' (inability to buy) rather than a decline in food availability
Trace the hyper-inflation of rice prices in 1943 and its impact on rural artisans and transport workers
Compare this historical event with a modern-day 'Starvation Death' report in a food-surplus state
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically evaluates the difference between 'Food Availability' and 'Food Access'
activities_projects.evaluation: Theoretical depth and quality of the entitlement analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: MSP, Procurement, and the 'Mountain of Grain'
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the fiscal and physical challenges of massive buffer stocks
steps: Evaluate the 'Carrying Cost' of buffer stocks that exceed the minimum norms (e.g., 80 million tonnes vs. 30 million tonnes norm)
Analyze the 'Open-Ended Procurement' policy and its impact on the government's fiscal deficit
Propose a solution for 'Grain Rot' in CAP (Cover and Plinth) storage facilities
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the economic inefficiencies in state-led food management
activities_projects.evaluation: Financial logic and feasibility of the storage solutions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The PDS Leakage Audit: Targeted vs. Universal
activities_projects.objective: Assess the 'Targeting Errors' in the Indian Rationing System
steps: Analyze the 'Inclusion Error' (non-poor getting BPL cards) and 'Exclusion Error' (eligible poor having no cards)
Investigate the 'Diversion of Grains' to the open market by Fair Price Shop (FPS) owners to recover low margins
Draft a policy brief on using 'Biometric Authentication' (Aadhaar) to minimize PDS leakages
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies systemic corruption and administrative hurdles in welfare delivery
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical insight into leakage mechanisms and reform proposals (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ecological Costs of the Green Revolution
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the long-term sustainability of the 'Wheat-Rice' monoculture
steps: Correlate the high MSP for wheat and rice with the depletion of the water table in the 'Bread Basket' states
Analyze the 'Soil Salinity' and 'Toxicity' caused by excessive chemical fertilizer use since the 1970s
Argue for the 'Diversification of Crops' toward millets (Nutri-cereals) as a climate-resilient food security strategy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the trade-off between immediate food security and long-term ecology
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific and economic integration in the sustainability report (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: National Food Security Act (NFSA) 2013 Legal Audit
activities_projects.objective: Examine the shift from 'Welfare' to a 'Rights-Based' approach
steps: Analyze the legal entitlement under NFSA: 5kg of foodgrains per person at subsidized prices (₹3/2/1 per kg)
Evaluate the 'Maternity Benefit' and 'Nutritional Standards' for children defined in the act
Discuss the role of 'Grievance Redressal Officers' in ensuring the right to food is upheld
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes economic policy with constitutional rights
activities_projects.evaluation: Knowledge of legal provisions and their economic impact (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Global Hunger Index (GHI) and 'Hidden Hunger'
activities_projects.objective: Investigate why India ranks low on GHI despite being food self-sufficient
steps: Define 'Hidden Hunger' (Micronutrient deficiency) and its presence in the Indian population
Analyze the four indicators of GHI: Undernourishment, Child Wasting, Child Stunting, and Child Mortality
Debate: 'Is India's problem a lack of Calories or a lack of Nutrition?'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically examines the 'Self-sufficiency' myth in the context of malnutrition
activities_projects.evaluation: Data-driven argumentation and nutritional awareness (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Political Economy of MSP
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how regional political interests shape national agricultural policy
steps: Investigate why MSP is effectively implemented in only a few states (Punjab, Haryana, AP)
Analyze the 'Market Distortions' created when the state acts as the sole buyer during harvest seasons
Evaluate the impact of high MSP on the 'Inflation' of food prices for the urban poor
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the intersection of politics, price support, and market dynamics
activities_projects.evaluation: Sophistication in identifying socio-political influences on economy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Food Security and Global Trade (WTO)
activities_projects.objective: Understand the conflict between national buffer stocks and international trade rules
steps: Research why the World Trade Organization (WTO) limits the 'Subsidies' India gives to its farmers
Explain the 'Green Box' and 'Amber Box' subsidies in the context of Indian agriculture
Draft an argument defending India's 'Public Stockholding for Food Security' at a mock WTO summit
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates domestic food policy to international trade law
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity on international trade jargon and national interest (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Role of ADS and Grain Banks: A Social Audit
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate community-led alternatives to the bureaucratic PDS
steps: Trace the success of 'Academy of Development Science' (ADS) in setting up NGO-managed grain banks
Analyze the 'Transaction Costs' of a localized grain bank versus the centralized FCI system
Assess if the 'Cooperative Model' can replace PDS in tribal or remote regions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates decentralization as a solution to food insecurity
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparative analysis of centralized vs. decentralized systems (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Strategic Food Security 2050 Masterplan
activities_projects.objective: Design a resilient food system for a future with 1.6 billion people
steps: Incorporate 'Climate-smart Agriculture', 'Vertical Farming', and 'Fortified Grains' into a national plan
Design a 'Digital PDS' using blockchain to track grain from the farm gate to the consumer's plate
Create a 'Nutrition Security' index that replaces the current 'Food Security' (calorie) index
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates holistic mastery of production, distribution, and nutrition
activities_projects.evaluation: Innovation, strategic foresight, and technical feasibility (10 marks)
